Also, presenter Paul Martin delves into the museum's extraordinary lace collection.

programme.title: The Third Man

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: As the 1980's draw to a close, `Top of the Pops' remains a broad church. We celebrate 1988 by cherry-picking the cream of the crop. The programme includes stand-out hits from Yazz, S'Express and Bomb the Bass, representing the growing popularity of house music, new Australian pop royalty Kylie Minogue and Jason Donovan, who ruled the charts along with teen stars Bros and Tiffany, soul legends Womack and Womack, the Irish mist of Enya and British reggae from Maxi Priest.

programme.title: Aftersun
programme.desc: Sophie reflects on the shared joy and private melancholy of a holiday she took with her father twenty years earlier. Memories real and imagined fill the gaps between as she tries to reconcile the father she knew with the man she didn't.

programme.title: The Capture
programme.desc: More video evidence of the attack emerges, isolating Carey even further from those around her. With pressure building to find the assassin, Carey's assertion that SO15 is compromised gains little traction. Convinced that an insidious conspiracy is unfolding at the heart of the establishment, Carey takes matters into her own hands and seeks an unlikely ally in Gemma Garland. Meanwhile, Noah Pierson finds his first day at work more surprising than he ever expected...

programme.title: Small Prophets

programme.title: This Is BBC TWO

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Adam Henson and Anita Rani head to Devon to look at all stages of lambing, the success of which can shape a farm's fortunes for months to come.

programme.title: Live: WA World Indoor Championship Athletics

programme.desc: Coverage of the 2026 World Indoor Championships from Toruń, Poland.

programme.desc: In Walmer, the crew respond to reports of a major incident involving a small boat in the Channel. On arrival, they are faced with mass casualties in the freezing water. Plus, in North Wales, the crew race to the rescue of a yacht.

programme.title: Forensics: The Real CSI
programme.desc: West Midlands and Cumbria Police join forces, determined to find forensics that will take down a brazen gang of ATM thieves who have been attacking cashpoints across the country.

programme.title: Training Day
programme.desc: Taut urban tale following a rookie cop's first day of training in an elite LAPD narcotics squad headed by a veteran policeman with an extreme, often morally questionable approach to law enforcement. The shell-shocked greenhorn is left to consider whether these unorthodox methods are an abuse of power or a necessary evil in a city overrun by violent crime.

programme.title: Victoria & Abdul
programme.desc: Abdul Karim arrives from India to participate in Queen Victoria's golden jubilee. The young clerk is surprised to find favour with the queen herself. As Victoria questions the constrictions of her long-held position, the two forge an unlikely and devoted alliance that her household and inner circle try to destroy. As their friendship deepens, the queen begins to see a changing world through new eyes, joyfully reclaiming her humanity.

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Features a studio audience quizzing top politicians on the events of the day. The panel surrounding presenter Fiona Bruce is composed of at least one member of the three major parties.

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The team's restorations include an exquisite Guyanese bangle, an electric guitar with a past, a leather tallit bag and a family's rediscovered set of Kent peg tiles.

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Contestants compete across the series, scoring points by answering questions correctly. However, they must avoid the impossible answers, otherwise they will be eliminated from the show until the next day.

programme.desc: The five remaining players from the second heat return to the Great Hall to battle it out for a place in the quarter-finals. The sense of trepidation is palpable as they face a mind-bending chess puzzle called 'the five queens'.

programme.title: Clive Myrie's Italian Roadtrip
programme.desc: Starting at his favourite holiday spot on the Amalfi coast, Clive goes inland to a mozzarella factory, where he discovers how to make everyone's favourite stringy cheese. Next, he heads to Capri, where he makes his wife Catherine a pair of the Capri sandals made famous by Jackie Kennedy and spends the evening singing Italian folk songs on stage at a club favoured by A-listers like Beyonce and Jay Z.

programme.title: Serengeti
programme.desc: The dry season is now upon the animals of the Serengeti, the toughest of times for some but a food bonanza for others. The increasing drought turns the waterhole into a death trap for the herds, but for the lions it's a gift and Kali and her cubs are finally able to enjoy life back in the heart of the pride.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Michael Aspel and a team of experts examine artefacts offered up by the public at Knebworth House. Items featured include a marine chronometer and a diamond brooch.

programme.title: Flog It!

Christina values a Victorian Claret jug and David gets all of a flutter over a large selection of antique fans. Meanwhile, Paul Martin visits Laugharne, the home of his literary hero, Dylan Thomas.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Former winners Amy Gledhill, Jacqui Joseph, Laurence Llewelyn-Bowen and Tim Vine test their general knowledge skills on this week's Richard Osman's House of Games: Champions.

programme.title: Global Eye

He lends a hand bringing in the cattle and hears how Rob Roy MacGregor might not have been the romantic figure of popular fiction. Was he a hero or just a cattle thief? At Rob Roy's graveside, Paul meets a man who casts doubts on the clansman's last resting place.

programme.title: Mastermind

programme.desc: After Debra forces Ray to do some charity work at a local hospital, he winds up enjoying himself because the patients treat him like he's a celebrity.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Frasier's high school reunion is approaching and he is afraid to go because he believes that he is cursed. Every time the high school reunion comes round, it coincides with a severe downturn in his life.

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Frasier suspects that his new girlfriend, Marie, gives him love and attention only for the free psychiatric advice.

Over dinner, Graham and Barbara relax into their roles, leaving Rachel Lonergan feeling guilty about having labelled them as game players.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Malcolm and Fiona Piers-Taylor have an alternative approach to their business at The Old Mill House B&B in Bridport, Dorset. Richard Tuke and Susan Hatch think it will be a wigwam, while Rachel and Dan Lonergan are expecting a commune.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: At the Brighton Inn, B&B newcomers Rachel and Dan Lonergan want to offer a luxury, boutique experience for their guests. After getting negative feedback on the last visit, Malcolm and Fiona Piers-Taylor go on the offensive.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: It's the final day of the competition and the B&Bers meet for one last time to confront their rivals. Tensions run high as the payments are revealed and the winner is announced.

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: Doug and Carrie want to get pregnant, but can't get Arthur out from under their feet. Doug hires an attractive dog walker to take him out.

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: Doug is sure he's found the perfect birthday gift for Carrie. However, he learns that you really shouldn't go for the discount offer when it comes to corrective laser eye surgery.

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: Doug tries to beat a record at work. The older members of the IPS depot aren't too happy that Doug is going to break the record held by their friend.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: When Ray drops off Ally for a sleepover, he gets the idea that his nemesis in the cookie drive is making a pass at him.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra gets annoyed with Ray when he seems pleased that she's picked one of her attractive friends to replace her if she dies.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra gives a bridal shower for Amy, but her good intentions get sidetracked by Marie's overzealous participation.

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Frasier mistakenly believes he is the object of a secret admirer while he is dating an attractive new woman.

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Maris repossesses Niles's car after his legal bills exceed his salary. He is forced to drive a small car and give up his apartment at the Montana for a tiny studio at the Shangri-La.

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Niles hosts a fashionable party at Maris's idyllic country retreat. Niels is desperately hoping to win the prestigious award which the dinner club hands out each year: the Golden Apron award.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Lisa meets a fellow horse-lover but then falls into a circle of snooty girls. Homer takes Marge on a romantic cruise.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: A charismatic new preacher comes to town and shakes things up at church. Reverend Lovejoy fights back by investigating his mysterious past.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: In Michigan, Reverend Lovejoy uncovers the reason why Bode left for Springfield, while the congregation must decide whether to banish their new priest when they learn of his crime.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The week begins at Becketts in Southsea, Portsmouth. The guests find the design delightful, but parking problems, a duvet disaster and a noisy night mean that host Terence's feedback isn't as flawless as expected.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: At The Westby Guest House in Boscombe, Duncan and Jessie have a systematic approach to running a B&B. Although the rooms are impressive, Duncan's strict house rules soon leave a sour taste.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Paul and Tracey host visit three of the week at Newell Restaurant and Rooms in Sherborne, Dorset. Terence is taken with the blissful bedding, but Duncan's substandard slumber and the communal coffee zone prove controversial.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: For the final visit of the week, the B&Bers embrace the great outdoors at Lydcott Glamping in Cornwall, hosted by Emma and Rich. All the guests are taken with the spectacular surroundings, but less so with their cabins and the DIY breakfast.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Dreadful duvets, bothersome beds and breakfast blues are all on the agenda as the B&Bers meet up on payment day to settle their differences. After a particularly emotional discussion, we find out which B&B has won the week.

programme.title: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or List It
programme.desc: In Hamilton, Kirstie and Phil dive headfirst into a domestic battleground with warring couple Scott and Jacqui. Their four-bedroom new-build, which they bought 14 years ago when their two daughters were still at home, now feels like a misfit.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: In north London, Kirstie Allsopp helps Digi find a future-proof home, while Phil Spencer's with Rebecca and Sohail, whose rental is worth £200k more than their budget to buy.

programme.title: Sleepless in Seattle
programme.desc: A recently engaged woman becomes obsessed with meeting the grieving widower she heard on a late-night radio show. Sam, the widower, receives hundreds of letters following the radio phone-in which was instigated by his son Jonah.

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Channel 4's flagship news programme. With sport and weather.

programme.title: Infinity Pool

programme.desc: Newly-weds Shariqa and Imani have struggled to keep their renovation project on schedule or on budget in Cambridgeshire. With just £10,000 to add a kitchen and transform the ground floor, Stacey has her work cut out.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond

programme.desc: The radio station goes from bad to worse without Roz and Frasier, and management is compelled to change the programming. Our two friends are certain that they will get their old jobs back.

programme.title: In Our Blood: The Forever Chemicals Scandal
programme.desc: In almost every respect, Bentham is a quintessential Yorkshire town: proud, close-knit and set against breathtaking countryside. Two years ago, an environmental journalist's investigation revealed it to be the most contaminated place ever recorded in the UK for PFAS - toxic so-called forever chemicals, some of which are linked to cancers and other serious illnesses. For decades, the town's main employer, Angus Fire, produced firefighting foam now believed to be the likely source of the pollution. The company routinely manufactured and tested this foam on site; the government ultimately banned it in 2025. This groundbreaking documentary reveals how those chemicals may have spread beyond the factory boundary and how a small town is coming to terms with what it means for them.
